channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Meteor over Massachusetts prompts reports of booms across US and Canada - The Guardian
item.description:
1. Meteor over Massachusetts prompts reports of booms across US and Canada  The Guardian
  2. Footage shows meteorite entering atmosphere  WJAR
  3. Meteor explodes over Massachusetts. What we know and where it landed  NBC Boston
  4. NASA says meteorite may be able to be found in Cape Cod Bay  Boston Herald
  5. Meteor explodes off coast of Massachusetts, causing loud boom  CBS News

item.title: May's Blue Moon wows stargazers worldwide — see these stunning photos of the smallest full moon of 2026 - Space
item.description:
1. May's Blue Moon wows stargazers worldwide — see these stunning photos of the smallest full moon of 2026  Space
  2. The Blue Moon rises tonight: Where and when to see the second full moon of May  Space
  3. Clear skies give Metro Detroit perfect Blue Moon viewing weather  ClickOnDetroit | WDIV Local 4
  4. Saturday’s micromoon will be the farthest, smallest and dimmest of the year : NASA  KOMO
  5. A rare blue micromoon arrives this weekend and won't be back until December 2028. Here's what to expect.  CBS News

item.title: Scientists Find Cosmic 'Rosetta Stone' To Decode Baffling Signals From Deep Space - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Scientists Find Cosmic 'Rosetta Stone' To Decode Baffling Signals From Deep Space  ScienceAlert
  2. Student astronomer discovers 'Rosetta Stone' for mysterious cosmic signals  Phys.org
  3. First Proof Mysterious Radio Pulses Come From Cataclysmic Variables Represents “Astronomical Rosetta Stone”  IFLScience
  4. Astronomers might have decoded a mysterious signal from space  The Independent
  5. Mysterious signals keep coming from space. We have found their ‘Rosetta stone’  The Conversation

item.title: Supermassive black holes could be the universe's biggest planet nurseries - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Supermassive black holes could be the universe's biggest planet nurseries  Phys.org
  2. Supermassive black holes could be prolific planet-making machines  New Atlas
  3. Supermassive black holes give rise to billions of planets  Universe Space Tech
  4. Millions of planets might form around supermassive black holes  New Scientist

item.title: In 2017, astronomers spotted the first object ever confirmed to have come from another star system passing through our own: a strange, elongated visitor called ‘Oumuamua that seemed to accelerate as it left, in a way scientists are still debating - Space Daily
item.description: In 2017, astronomers spotted the first object ever confirmed to have come from another star system passing through our own: a strange, elongated visitor called ‘Oumuamua that seemed to accelerate as it left, in a way scientists are still debating  Space Daily

item.title: About 63 light-years away there is a deep-blue world that looks deceptively like Earth from a distance, but on the planet HD 189733b the temperature reaches 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit and the winds scream at thousands of miles an hour - Space Daily
item.description: About 63 light-years away there is a deep-blue world that looks deceptively like Earth from a distance, but on the planet HD 189733b the temperature reaches 2,000 degrees Fahrenheit and the winds scream at thousands of miles an hour  Space Daily

item.description: A giant star may have destroyed itself in one of the universe's rarest explosions  Phys.org

item.title: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. A major global estimate put the planet’s tree count at about three trillion, while NASA gives the Milky Way’s star count as roughly 100 to 400 billion. - Space Daily
item.description: There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way. A major global estimate put the planet’s tree count at about three trillion, while NASA gives the Milky Way’s star count as roughly 100 to 400 billion.  Space Daily

item.title: Honeybees can recognize human faces — they can be trained to distinguish between individual humans by face and continue to recognize them across different viewpoints, despite having a brain smaller than the head of a pin - Space Daily
item.description: Honeybees can recognize human faces — they can be trained to distinguish between individual humans by face and continue to recognize them across different viewpoints, despite having a brain smaller than the head of a pin  Space Daily

item.title: Gessel gold hoard: A 3,300-year-old stash of gleaming treasures that's one of the largest Bronze Age hoards from Europe - Live Science
item.description: Gessel gold hoard: A 3,300-year-old stash of gleaming treasures that's one of the largest Bronze Age hoards from Europe  Live Science

item.title: There are about 20 quadrillion ants alive on Earth at any moment — enough that their combined biomass outweighs every wild bird and mammal on the planet combined, even though the often-repeated claim that ant biomass equals human biomass has been - Space Daily
item.description: There are about 20 quadrillion ants alive on Earth at any moment — enough that their combined biomass outweighs every wild bird and mammal on the planet combined, even though the often-repeated claim that ant biomass equals human biomass has been  Space Daily

item.title: Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization' - Space
item.description:
1. Astronomers discover a 'lost world' of black hole mergers: 'It's the astronomical equivalent of uncovering an ancient civilization'  Space
  2. Scientists Stunned: Two Black Holes Captured Together in a Historic First  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  3. Astrophysicists strike black gold with treasure trove of gravitational wave detections  Phys.org
  4. The ‘age of gravitational astronomy’ is here  Scientific American
  5. Gravitational waves confirm predicted black-hole mass gap and probe stellar nuclear physics  Nature

item.title: Human Brain Cells Grown on a Chip Level Up to Play 'Doom' - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Human Brain Cells Grown on a Chip Level Up to Play 'Doom'  ScienceAlert
  2. Researchers teach brain cells to play 'Doom'  Phys.org
  3. GPU is not the end game in AI, but biological neurons  Sebastian Barros Newsletter
  4. 🧠 Here is the first living 3d neural network for AI  Techno-Science.net
  5. Lab-Grown Brain Organoids Propel Advances in Biocomputer Technology  Bioengineer.org

item.title: Bolted to Pioneer 10 is a plaque showing two humans and a cosmic map back to Earth, while the spacecraft itself is now a silent ghost ship drifting toward Aldebaran, a star it will not pass for another two million years. - Space Daily
item.description: Bolted to Pioneer 10 is a plaque showing two humans and a cosmic map back to Earth, while the spacecraft itself is now a silent ghost ship drifting toward Aldebaran, a star it will not pass for another two million years.  Space Daily

item.title: One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does - Phys.org
item.description: One of our planets may be missing, and it could explain why the solar system looks the way it does  Phys.org

item.description: Stanford’s Revolutionary New Microscope Reveals Living Cells in Stunning Detail  SciTechDaily

item.title: CERN Finds High-Significance Hint of Physics Beyond Standard Model - Ground News
item.description:
1. CERN Finds High-Significance Hint of Physics Beyond Standard Model  Ground News
  2. These exotic particles could break physics  Scientific American
  3. Large Hadron Collider detects strange particle behavior that could rewrite physics  ScienceDaily
  4. At the world's largest particle accelerator, CERN physicists observed a particle behaving in a way that theory did not predict, the strongest indication so far of physics beyond what is currently known.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. ⚛️ An anomaly discovered at the LHC, contradicting the Standard Model of particle physics  Techno-Science.net

item.title: After a crippled seven-year journey Japan's Hayabusa probe limped home in 2010 and burned up in the sky over Australia, but not before releasing the first asteroid samples ever returned to Earth. - Space Daily
item.description: After a crippled seven-year journey Japan's Hayabusa probe limped home in 2010 and burned up in the sky over Australia, but not before releasing the first asteroid samples ever returned to Earth.  Space Daily

item.title: China Launches Artificial Human Embryos to See If They Can Develop in Space - Yahoo
item.description:
1. China Launches Artificial Human Embryos to See If They Can Develop in Space  Yahoo
  2. China Launches Human Embryo Experiment in Space  Newsweek
  3. Bypassing the 14-day rule: China’s ‘artificial embryos’ exploit a double loophole in orbit  WION
  4. Effects of Microgravity on Human Evolution  RaillyNews
  5. China Launched Artificial Embryos to Orbit to Find Out If We Can Have Space Babies  Gizmodo

item.title: We couldn't afford to pay for my mom's dementia assisted living anymore. She moved into a tiny house next door to me. - Business Insider
item.description: We couldn't afford to pay for my mom's dementia assisted living anymore. She moved into a tiny house next door to me.  Business Insider

item.title: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer - Space Daily
item.description: The Sun is not standing still. It is carrying the entire Solar System around the centre of the Milky Way, and one lap takes roughly 230 million years. The last time we were this far around the galaxy, Earth was in the Triassic Period and the very first dinosaurs wer  Space Daily

item.title: When Zhang Chenxing, who holds a PhD from MIT, co-founded Mega Engine Technology in Xi'an in early 2024, China's high-pressure oxygen-rich staged-combustion know-how sat almost entirely inside state propulsion houses — and by May 2026 his startu - Space Daily
item.description:
1. When Zhang Chenxing, who holds a PhD from MIT, co-founded Mega Engine Technology in Xi'an in early 2024, China's high-pressure oxygen-rich staged-combustion know-how sat almost entirely inside state propulsion houses — and by May 2026 his startu  Space Daily
  2. Chinese startup Mega Engine advances reusable staged-combustion rocket engine  SpaceNews
  3. China successfully fires reusable engine to compete with SpaceX  Interesting Engineering
  4. Chinese Startup Fires ‘Blazing’ Oxygen-Rich Engine for Deep Space Missions  Gizmodo

item.description: NASA’s Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter Has Snapped Its 100,000th Image of the Red Planet  My Modern Met

item.title: Strange Event Might Have Been A Primordial Black Hole Winking At Us - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Strange Event Might Have Been A Primordial Black Hole Winking At Us  ScienceAlert
  2. Something Just Passed Between Us and a Distant Star.  Universe Today
  3. An Unknown Object Passed Between Earth And A Star In The Large Magellanic Cloud, In An Event That Lasted An Hour  IFLScience
  4. A glow lasting only 1 hour observed in space in 2019 may have revealed a primordial black hole with a mass of 3 moons, associated with dark matter and considered 100,000 times more likely than common stellar objects by scientists in the study.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás
  5. A mysterious object passed in front of a distant star for one hour, and it may be a primordial black hole  MSN

item.title: 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'Astonishing': James Webb telescope spots the most chemically primitive galaxy in the ancient universe  Live Science
  2. The Galaxy That Forgot to Spin  Universe Today
  3. The James Webb Telescope photographed a gigantic galaxy less than 2 billion years after the Big Bang that simply does not rotate, a behavior astronomers only expected to see in much older galaxies.  CPG Click Petróleo e Gás

item.title: Is extracting oxygen from lunar soil the future of space exploration? - Phys.org
item.description: Is extracting oxygen from lunar soil the future of space exploration?  Phys.org

item.description:

  2. Astrophysicist Stan Woosley awarded two of astronomy's top prizes for seminal studies of supernovae  UC Santa Cruz - News
  3. Three scientists win Hong Kong’s Shaw Prize for developing therapy for rare leukaemia  thestar.com.my
  4. 7 scientists win Shaw Prize in 2026  China Daily - Global Edition
  5. Shaw Prize 2026 Honors Pioneers of Cancer Cures and Cosmic Origins  BriefGlance

item.title: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to roughly five times the temperature of the surface of the Sun, and it is the violent expansion of that superheated air that creates the crack of thunder. - Space Daily
item.description: A single bolt of lightning can heat the air around it to roughly five times the temperature of the surface of the Sun, and it is the violent expansion of that superheated air that creates the crack of thunder.  Space Daily

item.title: Scientists Spot What Appears to Be a Ring-Shaped "Planet Factory" Deep Out in Space - Futurism
item.description:
1. Scientists Spot What Appears to Be a Ring-Shaped "Planet Factory" Deep Out in Space  Futurism
  2. Scientists discover a giant “planet factory” beyond Jupiter  ScienceDaily
  3. Scientists Just Found the Solar System’s Original “Planet Factory”  SciTechDaily
  4. Cosmic “Planet Factory” discovered beyond Jupiter, say scientists  ODISHA RAY
  5. Jupiter Created the Birthplace of Rocky Bodies in the Early Solar System  Universe Today
